summary_en: ROE 8.0%+ by FY3/31 as final goal; FY3/27–FY3/29 mid-term plan designated as critical execution phase. Current ROE ~6% on operating earnings basis (excluding one-time gains). Three pillars: next-generation wholesale evolution, portfolio redesign, management foundation strengthening.
title_en: Notice of Mid-Term Management Plan Formulation and Revision of Measures to Realize Management with Awareness of Capital Costs and Stock Price
translation_en_brief: Suzuken has formulated a three-year mid-term management plan (FY3/27–FY3/29) and updated its capital-cost and stock-price-aware initiatives in response to exchange requirements. The plan designates ROE of 8.0%+ by FY3/31 as its ultimate target, with the current three-year window as a critical execution phase. The company's recent ROE of ~9% included one-time gains from policy-stock sales; underlying operating earnings basis ROE stands at ~6% and remains in improvement phase. Three strategic pillars frame the plan: (1) evolution to next-generation wholesale operations by combining traditional margin business with fee-based services (functionalization of capabilities); (2) portfolio redesign through internal evolution and external alliances, decoupling from legacy business frameworks to pursue higher-value models; (3) strengthening of management foundation through planned talent development, specialist hiring in DX and data analytics, and cross-group coordination. The company views the changing pharmaceutical and healthcare environment—including cost-containment policy, specialty drug ratio growth, and rising labor and logistics costs—as requiring fundamental business model transformation while maintaining stable supply as social infrastructure.
